item.text: Coordinating `PR581-009` through the required dispatcher first. I’m handing it the task metadata, report location, and the instruction to decide on specialists, wait for them, and converge with a complete result.

item.prompt:
You are the dispatcher for SocialPredict task PR581-009. Coordinate this task end-to-end in the target repo at /workspace/socialpredict/backend, following /workspace/spec-socialpredict-tasks/AGENTS.md. You must decide whether to spawn any specialist agents, ensure they wait before concluding, and keep work within the exact task scope from the metadata below.

Task metadata:
- task_id: PR581-009
- title: Align unchecked bets files with SRP
- working_dir: /workspace/socialpredict/backend
- report_dir: /workspace/socialpredict/.codex-reports/tasks/PR581-009
- report files already exist:
  - meta.json
  - summary.json
  - conversation.ndjson
  - decisions.ndjson
- use /workspace/spec-socialpredict-tasks/scripts/codex-report.py for deterministic report I/O when practical
- specialists append facts/decisions; dispatcher owns summary.json
- prefer incremental reads over rereading full logs
- if summary.json.context.handoff_requested becomes true, stop branching, record handoff, update summary, and converge

Scope constraints from prompt:
- Source of truth is the SRP checklist supplied by the user: only unchecked bets files belong in this task.
- internal/domain/bets/service.go is completed baseline; preserve it as owner of place/sell business rules.
- Preserve completed handler/service split for market bet history, buy, and sell flows.
- Work only in these files/symbols:
  - internal/domain/bets/errors.go: domain error vars, ErrDustCapExceeded, ErrDustCapExceeded.Error
  - internal/domain/bets/models.go: PlaceRequest, PlacedBet, SellRequest, SellResult
  - internal/domain/bets/service_helpers_test.go: stubMarketService.GetMarket, stubMarketService.GetUserPositionInMarket, gateClock.Now, TestMarketGate_Open, TestFeeCalculator_Calculate, TestBalanceGuard_EnsureSufficient, ledgerRepo.Create, ledgerRepo.UserHasBet, ledgerUsers.GetUser, ledgerUsers.ApplyTransaction, TestBetLedger_ChargeAndRecord, TestBetLedger_ChargeAndRecord_RollsBackOnRepoError, TestBetLedger_CreditSale, TestSaleCalculator_Calculate
  - internal/domain/bets/service_test.go: fakeRepo.Create, fakeRepo.UserHasBet, fakeMarkets.GetMarket, fakeMarkets.GetUserPositionInMarket, fakeUsers.GetUser, fakeUsers.ApplyTransaction, fixedClock.Now, TestServicePlace_Succeeds, TestServicePlace_InsufficientBalance, TestServicePlace_InvalidOutcome, TestServicePlace_MarketClosed, TestServiceSell_Succeeds, TestServiceSell_NoPosition, TestServiceSell_DustCapExceeded, TestServiceSell_RequestTooSmall
- Goal: update every listed unchecked symbol to align with Single Responsibility Principle without moving business rules back into handlers.

Requirements:
- Start by assessing whether specialists are needed; prefer the smallest useful set.
- If you spawn specialists, wait for them before concluding.
- Make or coordinate the needed code changes in the target repo.
- Run appropriate verification for the touched scope.
- Update the canonical report summary.json before concluding.
- Return a concise completion summary with changed files, checks, assumptions, and any follow-up.

item.text: The dispatcher is running with full task scope and report ownership requirements. I’m waiting on its coordination result now, then I’ll finalize the task outcome and report the concrete changes and checks.

019d5717-ab80-7981-9477-e8bfd785e0f1.message:
task_id: `PR581-009`  
status: `blocked`

I explicitly spawned the required `dispatcher_agent`, then converged on a blocked checkpoint without spawning specialists because the task’s allowed scope does not exist in this checkout. The repo at `/workspace/socialpredict` is on `main`, and there is no `backend/internal/domain/bets/` tree or the listed symbols/files (`errors.go`, `models.go`, `service_helpers_test.go`, `service_test.go`) anywhere under that path. Editing the older `backend/handlers/bets/...` code would violate the task’s source-of-truth scope and bypass the `PR581-004` dependency.

files_changed: `[]`

checks_run:
- `python3 /workspace/spec-socialpredict-tasks/scripts/codex-report.py read-summary --report-dir /workspace/socialpredict/.codex-reports/tasks/PR581-009`
- `find /workspace/socialpredict/backend -maxdepth 3 -type d`
- `rg -n "ErrDustCapExceeded|PlaceRequest|SellResult|fakeRepo|stubMarketService" /workspace/socialpredict/backend`
- `git -C /workspace/socialpredict branch --show-current`

I updated the canonical report summary at `/workspace/socialpredict/.codex-reports/tasks/PR581-009/summary.json` and appended a dispatcher decision/event recording the scope mismatch.

follow_ups:
- Check out or merge the missing dependency branch/content for `PR581-004` so `backend/internal/domain/bets/...` exists in `/workspace/socialpredict`.
- Re-run `PR581-009` against that checkout; no in-scope code change was possible in the current repository state.

item.text:
task_id: `PR581-009`  
status: `blocked`

The required `dispatcher_agent` was spawned first and completed coordination. No code changes were made because the in-scope target files do not exist in the current checkout at `/workspace/socialpredict`: `backend/internal/domain/bets/errors.go`, `models.go`, `service_helpers_test.go`, and `service_test.go` are absent, and `backend/internal/domain/bets/` is missing entirely. The repo is currently on branch `main`, so proceeding in older `backend/handlers/bets/...` code would violate the task’s stated scope and the `PR581-004` dependency.

files_changed: `[]`

checks_run: `python3 /workspace/spec-socialpredict-tasks/scripts/codex-report.py read-summary --report-dir /workspace/socialpredict/.codex-reports/tasks/PR581-009`, `find /workspace/socialpredict/backend -maxdepth 3 -type d`, `rg -n "ErrDustCapExceeded|PlaceRequest|SellResult|fakeRepo|stubMarketService" /workspace/socialpredict/backend`, `git -C /workspace/socialpredict branch --show-current`

follow_ups: `Check out or merge the missing PR581-004 content so backend/internal/domain/bets/... exists, then rerun PR581-009.`
